rulings.Case Title: OZ OPTICS, LIMITED, A CANADIAN CORPORATION et al VS. LAW OFFICES OF JAMES M. BRADEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 12, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT JAMES BRADEN, LAW OFFICES OF JAMES M. BRADEN DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The Court grants defendant's request for judicial notice. The Court overrules the demurrer in its entirety. The corporate plaintiffs did not need to obtain a certificate of qualification to file the instant suit. Corporations Code § 191 excludes the maintenance or defense of a lawsuit from the definition of intrastate business. Filing a lawsuit is part of the maintenance process. Further, judicial notice of a "screen shot" from the Secretary of State does not establish the truth of those matters. As for the conduct of other intrastate business sought to be established by judicial notice of various matters asserted in prior lawsuits, the truth of such matters cannot be established by judicial notice. Plaintiff Sezerman sufficiently states a cause of action for legal malpractice and a factual issue remains regarding whether he was damaged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/RK

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD ELMS VS. LEAH AHN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Leah Ahn'S Motion Of Court'S Inherent Power To Correct Its Error
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 12, 2013, Line Defendant Leah Ahn'S Motion Of Court'S Inherent Power To Correct Its Error The motion for reconsideration under CCP 1008 is denied as being untimely. The request that the Court on its own motion reconsider its May 23, 2013, decision is denied. The Court notes by way of further explanation of the May 23, 2013, order: (1) In Wools, the Petitioner (Wools) objected to the arbitration before it commenced and had filed a motion to stay the arbitration which was pending when the arbitration took place. Here, Ms. Ahn requested the arbitration, participated in it without objection, and only raised her objection under Business & Professions Code § 7191 when an adverse result was rendered. Therefore, for the purpose of statutory interpretation, the arbitration clause was not "enforced against" her but rather both parties voluntarily participated. (2) Wools does not obviate the concept of waiver under cases cited by Mr. Elms. Wools only states that because Mr. Wools was an attorney, he cannot be said to have waived section 7191. See Wools, 127 Cal.App.4th at 214. (3) Further, Ms. Ahn did not meet her burden of proof to establish that the subject contract failed to comply with section 7191. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/RK

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND SARNO VS. SEARIVER MARITIME INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Seariver Martime Inc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 12, 2013, Line Defendant Seariver Martime Inc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication Defendant SeaRiver Maritime, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of the request for punitive damages pertaining to the Jones Act and unseaworthiness claims are denied. Defendant cannot seek piecemeal adjudication of punitive damages. (CCP § 437c(f); Catalano v. Sup.Ct. (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 91, 97.) rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND SARNO VS. SEARIVER MARITIME INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Seariver Martime Inc Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 12, 2013, Line Defendant Seariver Martime Inc Motion For Summary Adjudication Defendant SeaRiver Maritime, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of the negligence per se claim is denied. Defendant has not met his initial burden of production of evidence. Defendant does not address all regulatory violations identified in Plaintiff's response to Form Interrogatory no. 14.1 (i.e. 33 CFR §§ 96.230 and 96.240). The existence of any single asserted regulatory violation would raise an issue of material fact for trial. rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND SARNO VS. SEARIVER MARITIME INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Seariver Maritime Inc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 12, 2013, Line Defendant Seariver Maritime Inc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication Defendant SeaRiver Maritime, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of the Jones Act and unseaworthiness claims are denied. Defendant has failed to meet its burden of production of evidence to show that there is no issue of material fact as to i) whether plaintiff suffered physical injury and ii) if not, whether plaintiff was in the "zone of danger" for purposes of the applicable test set forth in the parties' papers. (See Consolidated Rail Corp. v. Gottshall (1994) 512 U.S. 532, 556.) In reply, Defendant refers to material facts and evidence that were not addressed in its moving separate statement and has thus demonstrated that there are triable issues of fact. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SAAM ARYAN VS. AVIS BUDGET GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And Req. For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 12, 2013, Line 1: Defendant AVIS RENT A CAR SYSTEM LLC'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT F. HANDELMAN VS. LORETTA BOZUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 12, 2013, Line 20, Application For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment Plaintiff's application for right to attach is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff has failed to include a memorandum of points and authorities, which is required under CRC 3.1113. (See also CRC 3.1114.) Plaintiff's application for right to attach also does not describe the categories of property adequately to allow Defendant to identify the specific property sought to be attached. (See CCP § 484.020(e).) rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. DEIRDRE STACK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 12, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT DEIRDRE STACK DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer based upon CCP 430.10(e) is overruled. Plaintiff states a cause of action for breach of contract and identifies when the assignment occurred. The demurrer based on CCP 430.10(g) is overruled. Defendant is not permitted to file a special demurrer in this limited civil case. See CCP 92(c). In addition, a liberal reading of the complaint indicates that the parties entered into a written contract and plaintiff sufficiently alleges the contract according to its legal effect. rulings.Case Title: MANUEL AUSTIN et al VS. MARK ALEX BOLLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 12, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Overruled as to the first cause of action, and sustained, with 10 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action. For the first cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege a special relationship between Plaintiff and Defendant's employees. (See Zelig v. County of Los Angeles (2002) 27 Cal.4th 1112, 1129.) For the second cause of action, Plaintiffs rely on general allegations, which are insufficient. (Cerna v. City of Oakland (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 1340, 1347.) Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend the second cause of action, if they can do so in good faith, to specifically allege in what manner the condition constituted a dangerous condition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MISSION WALK HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, VS. BERRY STREET, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Stay Of Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 12, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT BERRY STREET, LLC, BRIDGE HOMES, INC., ROBERTS-OBAYASHI CORPORATION Mtn To Stay Of Proceedings The motion is denied. This case was stayed on July 19, 2013 per the order of Judge Goldsmith. The issues raised in this motion, as well all other appropriate matters should brought in front of the arbitrator. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/RK

rulings.Case Title: LFG NATIONAL CAPITAL, LLC VS. JOSEPH M. ALIOTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment; Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 12, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF LFG NATIONAL CAPITAL, LLC Notice Of Hearing And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment; The application for a Writ of Attachment as to Defendant Alioto Law Firm is denied. Under C.C.P. § 483.015(b)(4), any attachment upon plaintiff's claim must be reduced by the value of any security interest in the property of the defendant held plaintiff to secure its indebtedness. Here, plaintiff's claim is secured by all of the property of the defendant law firm, including the property sought to be attached. The plaintiff is entitled to the alternatively requested writ of possession. Plaintiff has met its burden of demonstrating the probable validity of its claim against defendant law firm under C.C.P. §§ 512.010 and 512.060. Plaintiff must satisfy the under taking requirements of C.C.P. § 515.010. As for the defendants' Motion to Disqualify filed on August 2, 2103 and set for hearing August 30, 2013, appearances are required to determine the impact of this motion on the request relief. The Court believes that should it be appropriate to delay the issuance of the Writ of Possession until the resolution of the Motion to Disqualify, then it would be appropriate to at this time issue a Temporary Restraining Order under C.C.P. § 513.010 cover the acts specified in C.C.P. § 513.010 during the interim. As for Plaintiff's application for Writ of Attachment against the individual defendant, hearing is required. Among other issues, the parties should be prepared to address the issue of whether the property sought to be attached is the property of the individual defendant. All arguments regarding the claim of exemption are moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/RK
